accept: act help with no args prints a comprehensive agent-onboarding tutorial covering: op-log model, canonical work loop, identity/claim, when to use composed MCP tools, when to file follow-ups, where concurrency resolves automatically.
act --help still prints the terse one-line subcommand list (existing behavior).
Tutorial includes copy-pastable example sequences a new agent can adapt.
Tutorial references the commit_marker convention for orphan-close detection.
Tutorial is short enough an agent can absorb it in one read (target ~200 lines), long enough to actually onboard (no hand-waving).
act help <topic> for at least 'workflow' and 'ops-model' (others optional v0.2).
payload.description:
Today act --help and act help both emit a one-line list of subcommands. New projects need a single self-contained command they can run to learn the entire workflow: how the op-log model works, what the canonical work loop looks like (act_next -> work -> commit with marker -> act_close), how identity and claim semantics work, when to file follow-ups, how concurrency resolves, what each subcommand is for at a high level (with deep flag reference still on per-subcommand --help). The bar: a fresh agent in a fresh repo can run act help and learn enough to start being useful, with no other docs.

Suggested split: act --help stays terse (subcommand list, conventional). act help (no flags) is the rich tutorial. act help <topic> (e.g. workflow, ops-model, mcp, identity) deeper dives.

This is the discoverable affordance every agent already knows to try. Tutorial-as-help-text is version-locked to the binary, can't drift, no separate doc to maintain in another file.
payload.title: act help should be a complete agent-onboarding tutorial
